channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Bloober Team’s Next Game Is a Star Trek Thriller Adventure Starring Ro Laren, Arriving 2027 on PS5, Xbox, and PC
item.content:encoded:
The newly established Paramount Games Studio has unveiled Star Trek: Shadow Frontier, a story-driven third-person action-adventure game in development at Silent Hill 2 remake studio Bloober Team, during the IGN Live Showcase 2026.

Arriving in 2027 for PlayStation 5, Xbox Series S|X, and PC, Star Trek: Shadow Frontier tells the story of Ro Laren, a Bajoran character played by actress Michelle Forbes, who returns to her iconic role from Star Trek: The Next Generation and Star Trek: Picard. After crashing onto a remote planet dominated by an unusual consciousness, Ro Laren must navigate a landscape that is as stunning as it is deadly.

In classic Star Trek style, Ro’s mission is fueled by a sense of duty and purpose. However, here, each discovery comes with a heavy price. The more she learns, the more she becomes ensnared in a twisted maze where her memories distort and the planet threatens to cut her off from reality. Only by unraveling the enigmas of this bizarre world can Ro hope to confront the shadows of her past, bravely venturing into the depths of darkness like never before.

A planet-wide entity has taken control of the terrain and turns it into a weapon. Creatures born from this environment patrol the area and strike unexpectedly, forcing Ro into frantic battles and narrow escapes. Every encounter will challenge her determination as much as her abilities. She must remain vigilant, observe her surroundings, and decide whether to engage in combat or evade her foes.

The hostile ecosystem constantly endangers Ro’s life. It aims to consume her consciousness, digging deep into her mind and tormenting her with disturbing visions. Environmental dangers and obstacles lie in wait at every turn. Exploration is both her sole route to salvation and a potential path to her downfall. Ro Laren can utilize iconic Star Trek technology to gain an edge, employing the Tricorder to scan and analyze objects and depending on the reliable Phaser to solve puzzles and fend off adversaries.

Bloober Team promised a balanced and diverse gameplay experience that combines exploration, puzzles, combat, and cinematic moments into a captivating, narrative-driven adventure that rewards patience, keen observation, and wise decision-making.

Shawn Kittelsen, SVP, Head of Creative and Production at Paramount Games Studio, said in a statement:

We’ve long admired the way Bloober Team crafts deeply immersive, atmospheric experiences that keep players on the edge of their seats. For Star Trek: Shadow Frontier, it was essential to partner with a studio that could both honor that legacy and push it forward. We have full confidence in Bloober’s ability to deliver a game that does justice to the Star Trek name while bringing something bold and new to the beloved franchise.

Piotr Babieno, CEO of Bloober Team, added:

We’ve been waiting a long time to finally share what we’ve been working on. As lifelong fans of science fiction, Star Trek has always held a special place in our hearts. By combining that passion with what we do best, horror, we’ve partnered with Paramount to create something truly unique: a new adventure set in a beloved universe, enriched with our own signature layer of dark, psychological thriller. We’re genuinely excited about this journey, and our hope is to honor the legacy of Star Trek in a way that feels both respectful yet refreshingly different.

The game is already available for wishlisting on Steam, and the page also includes preliminary PC system requirements:

  • Processor: AMD Ryzen 2700
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: Nvidia GTX 1660

By the way, yesterday Bloober Team also announced Cronos: Lazarus, a combat-focused DLC coming this Fall to Cronos: The New Dawn.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/bloober-team-star-trek-thriller-ro-laren-2027/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Ex-BioWare Devs Just Showed 20 Minutes of EXODUS, and Mass Effect Fans Are Going to Feel Very Seen
item.content:encoded:
During yesterday's Future Game Show 2026, Archetype Entertainment provided the biggest EXODUS gameplay preview yet. The game is shaping up well, aside from a few performance issues, and will definitely have Mass Effect fans eager to check it out. The inspiration has been clear from the start, after all, with former BioWare developers founding Archetype and contributing heavily to the game.

Players will take on the role of Jun Aslan, a lowly salvager living on the outskirts of Lidon who was orphaned from a once-powerful family. Jun carries a unique and mysterious genetic trait that allows him to interact with ancient Celestial technology, and it may just be the key to saving his homeworld.

The central threat is the Rot, a Celestial virus driving humanity toward extinction that doesn't just decay but intelligently and adaptively rewrites Celestial programming, almost as if it is alive. Jun and his crew must travel across the Centauri Cluster, exploring ancient ruins and deadly planets in search of powerful alien Remnants that could provide a cure.

Players can customize Jun as male or female with multiple appearance options. At the beginning of EXODUS, players choose an initial alignment: either a virtuous Paladin or an ambitious Immortal. This choice fundamentally changes how the world responds to Jun, and also directly ties into progression; the alignment chosen determines which abilities can be upgraded and how powers evolve over time. Later in the video, it is shown that key choices will contribute to either alignment options, and is thus highly reminiscent of Mass Effect's classic Paragon and Renegade dichotomy.

As we already covered in previous articles, one of the most distinctive narrative mechanics in the game is time dilation. Traveling at near-lightspeed across vast distances means days for Jun and their companions translate to weeks or months back home. What players prioritize, which Remnants they bring back, and how long they take on missions all affect how the world and people left behind have changed when they return.

The gameplay preview introduces Persepolis, the primary hub city on Lidon, to which Jun will return after an expedition. It is described as a major city where humans and Awakened species coexist, with merchants, soldiers, and medics from both communities. Players use it to take on new missions, upgrade gear, and see how their choices have reshaped the world around them. By the way, the Awakened in the world of EXODUS are highly intelligent animals brought on ark ships, the product of bioengineering and cybernetics, who now speak, think, and live fully alongside humans in society. They are not just companions; they run vendor stalls, serve as mercenaries and medics, and are woven into every layer of the world.

The showcase introduced six companions:

  • Phaedra Nath — a renowned xeno-archaeologist whose story is tangled with the Celestials; the relationship between her and Jun evolves and shapes both their futures
  • Tom Vargas — an experienced Traveler, cautious and morally grounded, who helps teach Jun the ropes
  • Elise Charroux — an impatient mech pilot who leans into brute force and loves to blow things up
  • Salt — an Awakened octopus mercenary, deadly and intelligent, with a strictly contractual attitude to loyalty
  • Houston — another Awakened companion and Jun's friend from the beginning of the story
  • C.C. Orlev — a mysterious space cowboy voiced by Matthew McConaughey, described as enigmatic; seen giving Jun a Recycler upgrade during the showcase

We also learned a lot more about combat. The aforementioned Recycler is a versatile weapon that shifts between Repeater mode for steady mid-range fire, Shredder mode for close-quarters impact, and Piercer mode for long-range precision. Jun also eventually comes into possession of The Gauntlet, a powerful artifact passed down from Jun's father that upgrades as Jun salvages alien technology. Abilities shown include:

  • Eruption — launches enemies and shapes livestone terrain
  • Lance — piercing strikes that clear Rot-produced bramble
  • Precognition — marks enemy locations
  • Scramble Cloak — enables stealth attacks
  • Sonic Lure — draws enemies into position

How players approach combat is entirely up to them, with stealth being a valid option, unlike in the Mass Effect games. Indeed, choices are a central pillar of EXODUS. The showcase showed a tense moment in which Jun must choose between Elise, who wants to vent the airlock to clear enemies in one shot, and Tom, who warns that innocent dock workers may be inside. These decisions ripple through the crew, the story, and the lives of people back home. Some will play out immediately, while others will take time to show their impact.

EXODUS is currently in the final stretch of development and set to launch on the PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, Steam, and Epic Games Store in early 2027, as previously announced by the studio.

About the author: With over two decades of experience in gaming journalism, Alessio Palumbo has led the gaming vertical at Wccftech since August 2015. He started working at a young age for Italian websites like Everyeye.it, Gamestar.it, Nextgame.it, and Multiplayer.it before kickstarting the indie English-language publication Worlds Factory as its founder and Editor in Chief. In the last decade, he has coordinated the overall output of Wccftech's gaming section, managed PR relations, assigned reviews, produced daily news coverage, edited gaming content as needed, and delivered game reviews. Arguably, his trademark content is the long series of exclusive developer interviews that have been cited by Wikipedia and by the biggest news media and gaming publications. His passion for technology also makes him knowledgeable when it comes to gaming hardware and tech. His favorite genres include RPGs, MMORPGs, and action/adventure games.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/exodus-20-minutes-gameplay-mass-effect-fans/
